reason.BSc / BEng / BCom in CS or IT (3-year): A 3-year computing degree maps cleanly to skilled-worker visas in the UK, Germany, Canada and Australia.
reason.BTech / BSc Honours / 4-year IT degree: A 4-year IT degree is RQF 6 plus and clears the degree-level bar for every major skilled-worker route.
reason.Master / PhD in computing: A postgraduate computing qualification is the strongest credential profile and helps with points-based routes.
reason.National Diploma in IT: A National Diploma may need an ECA or Ecctis equivalence to be read as degree-level abroad, but skills carry most of the weight in tech.
reason.Industry certifications only (AWS, Azure: Certifications without a degree still work for Germany IT-specialist and UK Skilled Worker where the role is degree-level, so demonstrable skills matter more here.
reason.Matric (NSC) + self-taught: Self-taught without a degree limits some points-based routes, but Germany IT-specialist accepts experience in place of a degree.
reason.Bootcamp graduate: A bootcamp alone rarely satisfies degree-equivalent checks, so lean on certifications and a strong portfolio.
reason.Other non-IT degree: A non-IT degree can still anchor a skilled-worker visa if your role is degree-level, paired with IT certifications.
reason.Currently studying: Finishing your qualification first will open more visa routes and stronger employer sponsorship.

reason.AWS (any level): An AWS certification is a globally recognised signal that shortens employer shortlisting for cloud and backend roles.
reason.Microsoft Azure: An Azure certification carries strong weight with the many destination employers running Microsoft stacks.
reason.Google Cloud (GCP): A GCP certification is well regarded for cloud and data roles abroad.
reason.Kubernetes / CKA / CKAD: A Kubernetes certification is a sought-after DevOps credential that employers actively screen for.
reason.CISSP / CISM / CEH: A senior security certification is in acute global demand and often unlocks a salary premium.
reason.Cisco (CCNA / CCNP / CCIE): A Cisco certification is solid evidence for networking and infrastructure roles abroad.
reason.CompTIA (Security+, Network+, etc.): CompTIA certifications are a useful baseline, strongest when paired with a degree-level role.
reason.Salesforce: A Salesforce certification opens a steady niche market across the UK, Ireland and Canada.
reason.PMP / PRINCE2: A project-management certification supports technical lead and delivery roles abroad.
reason.Scrum / SAFe: An agile certification is a helpful add-on but rarely the deciding credential on its own.
reason.Other / none: No headline certification recorded, so an in-demand cloud or security cert would noticeably strengthen your applications.

rules.empty_reason: No industry certifications recorded. A cloud certification (AWS, Azure, GCP) or a security certification (CISSP, CISM) is a fast, recognised way to strengthen overseas applications.

reason.Software developer / engineer: Software engineering is the highest-demand category abroad and is degree-level for UK, German and Canadian skilled-worker routes.
reason.Cloud architect / DevOps / SRE: Cloud, DevOps and SRE skills are among the most acute global shortages and command priority hiring.
reason.Cybersecurity specialist: Cybersecurity is a worldwide shortage area with strong sponsorship appetite and salary premiums.
reason.Data engineer / data scientist: Data engineering and data science are in heavy demand across all your target markets.
reason.ML / AI engineer: ML and AI engineering is a fast-growing, high-leverage specialism that employers actively sponsor.
reason.Database administrator: Database administration is steady, in-demand work that maps to degree-level skilled-worker roles.
reason.Web / multimedia developer: Web development has solid demand abroad, strongest when backed by a public portfolio.
reason.Mobile developer: Mobile development has reliable demand, particularly where you show shipped apps.
reason.Network engineer / sysadmin: Networking and sysadmin roles are in good demand, often paired with Cisco or cloud certifications.
reason.QA / test engineer: QA and test engineering has steady demand, strongest with automation experience.
reason.IT business analyst / systems designer: Technical analyst roles are workable but more competitive than core engineering, so lean on domain depth.
reason.Product / project manager (technical): Technical product and project roles hire well when paired with delivery track record and a recognised certification.
reason.IT support / help desk (technician): IT support is the hardest tech route abroad and the UK only accepts it via the Temporary Shortage List, which expires 31 December 2026.
reason.Other: Confirm how your role maps to destination shortage lists, since demand varies sharply by specialism.

rules.empty_reason: No IT discipline recorded. The strongest leverage abroad is in software, cloud or DevOps, cybersecurity and data or AI roles, so record your primary specialism.

ranges.reason: Under 2 years of experience limits sponsorship appetite, though Germany IT-specialist counts experience over a degree.
2 to 5 years of experience meets the bar for most skilled-worker sponsorship abroad.
5 to 10 years of experience makes you a strong applicant for competitive roles.
10+ years of experience positions you for senior and staff-level roles abroad.

reason.Active interviews with destination emplo: Live interviews with destination employers put you close to a sponsoring offer.
reason.Remote-first offer from destination empl: An offer from a destination employer is the strongest position and often the gateway to sponsorship.
reason.Currently remote for SA employer only: Remote work for an SA employer builds relevant track record, but you still need a destination offer to anchor a visa.
reason.Not yet — just exploring: No live offer yet, so the next step is targeting destination employers who sponsor.

reason.Valid — 2+ years remaining: Passport valid, which meets typical work-visa requirements.
reason.Valid — under 2 years remaining: Passport valid but renew soon, as most work visas need 2+ years remaining.
reason.Expired: Passport expired, so renew it before applying.
reason.No passport: No passport, so apply immediately as this is the first blocker.

reason.Current (within 6 months): Current SAPS clearance, which meets the visa-ready bar.
reason.Older than 6 months: Clearance older than 6 months, so most visa applications will need a fresh one timed to lodgement.
reason.Applied / in progress: Clearance in progress, which is the right move as SAPS turnaround is typically 4 to 8 weeks.
reason.None: No police clearance, so apply now as visa lodgement will require it.

reason.Under R50k: Below the typical R60k to R150k IT relocation budget, so build a buffer before lodging.
reason.R50k – R100k: Covers a lean single relocation but tight once a Germany Opportunity Card blocked account is in play.
reason.R100k – R200k: A comfortable relocation budget for most destinations.
reason.R200k – R400k: A strong financial position that covers a blocked-account route and family relocation.
reason.R400k+: An excellent financial cushion for any destination, including family moves.
reason.Prefer not to say: Budget not declared, so share it privately with us if it shapes your options.

reason.Native / first language: English as a first language clears the language bar for the UK, Ireland and Canada.
reason.Fluent: Fluent English is typically sufficient for English-language destinations.
reason.Conversational: Conversational English will need a test result, as UK Skilled Worker requires CEFR B2 from 8 January 2026.

reason.IELTS Academic: IELTS Academic is widely accepted, though confirm the exact variant your destination needs.
reason.IELTS for UKVI (UK Skilled Worker — requ: IELTS for UKVI matches the UK Skilled Worker requirement from 8 January 2026.
reason.IELTS General Training (Canada Express E: IELTS General Training is the accepted test for Canada Express Entry.
reason.PTE Academic: PTE Academic works for several destinations but is not accepted for Canada Express Entry.
reason.TOEFL: TOEFL is accepted by many destination employers, so confirm it for your specific route.
reason.None yet: No English test on record, which UK and Canada routes will require.

ranges.reason: No dependants is the easiest profile to relocate, with no dependant visas needed.
One dependant adds a partner or child visa step but is straightforward in most destinations.
Two or three dependants add visa cost and schooling logistics, so budget for them early.
Four or more dependants raise relocation cost and visa complexity, so specialist planning helps.

reason.As soon as possible: An ASAP timeline means you can start employer applications now.
reason.Within 6 months: A 6-month horizon is realistic once documents and a test are in hand.
reason.Within 1 year: A 1-year horizon is ideal for credential checks and visa processing.
reason.Within 2 years: A 2-year horizon gives room to add certifications and target the strongest route.
reason.Just exploring: Exploring only, so you can plan strategically without time pressure.
